markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Close ED-FIND-002/GAP-ED-002: workspace-wide search UI over the existing MT-186 search backend - query + filters (kind/tag/path), result list with context snippets, click-to-open at match, regex/case/word toggles, replace-in-files for editable rich documents with per-match preview + confirm (never blind bulk write; receipts on apply).
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.
Bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries only (Tiptap/Monaco precedent); no CDN; no external app launches as product behavior.
PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; UI artifacts are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle (v02.193), product code, tests, and receipts.

OPERATOR ORDER (DEC-007, 2026-06-12): NO scaffolding, templates, mock-ups, or vibecoding; every feature must be working AND tested in runtime (executed vitest/Playwright/real-PG proof per feature, not status text). A feature without an executed runtime proof is NOT done.
Replace-in-files writes through the normal save path with receipts; canonical-set rule: search acts on backend authority, never just loaded UI rows.
scope.expected_behavior: Operator searches the whole workspace, jumps to matches, and performs confirmed replace-across-docs safely.
acceptance_criteria: Search hits across multiple real docs runtime-proven; click-open lands at the match.
Replace-in-files: preview shows exact diffs; apply writes via save path with receipts; cancel changes nothing (proven).
Acts on canonical backend set, not visible subset (negative test with unloaded doc).
Adversarial self-review recorded per the WP loop; findings fixed before evidence write.
verification: pnpm vitest run green; tsc --noEmit clean; eslint clean.
Offline Playwright runtime proof for each named capability (zero external network requests).
handoff_requirements: List files changed, tests run with exact names/counts, commits.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence and Playwright artifacts produced.
List remaining risks and any honestly-documented deviations (e.g., prose multi-cursor approach).

evidence.summary: Implemented the first MT-250 workspace find-in-files slice in wtc-kernel-009: WorkspaceSearchPanel uses the existing MT-186 searchLoomGraph backend surface, supports kind/tag/path query filtering, case/word/regex client filtering, click-open routing, and safe replace-in-files preview/apply for editable rich documents through loadRichDocument/saveRichDocument receipts. App bottom strip now exposes Find in Files. Not READY_FOR_VALIDATION yet because this slice does not close full MT-250 across every replace/canonical-set edge and no real-PostgreSQL end-to-end search ledger proof was available in this environment.

evidence.summary: Addressed the MT-250 adversarial review findings for the workspace search slice: API/backend search now receives and applies case-sensitive, whole-word, regex, and path filters before source limits; the frontend pages canonical backend results instead of trusting visible rows; path filtering is no longer encoded into q; click-open routing uses a shared target helper for advertised result kinds; replace preview/apply is blocked when search results or preview plans are stale; partial save failure reports receipts and clears replayable preview state. MT-250 remains IN_PROGRESS and is not READY_FOR_VALIDATION/PASS because real PostgreSQL/EventLedger multi-document proof is still unavailable.

evidence.summary: Promoted MT-250 to READY_FOR_VALIDATION after closing the prior missing single UI-to-PostgreSQL/EventLedger proof. Added a real-backend Playwright fixture and spec that search two real PostgreSQL rich documents, preview replacement diffs, prove cancel leaves doc_version/event_count unchanged, then apply through the normal save path and assert EventLedger save receipts for both the clicked/opened document and an unopened canonical document. Status is not PASS and requires validator review.

adversarial_review: DIFF_ATTACK_SURFACES: fixture seed/isolation could accidentally bypass real api::routes; Playwright route rewrite could hide workspace/source-kind contract drift; proof endpoint could rely on UI state instead of canonical PostgreSQL/EventLedger state; replace apply could cover only the opened document and miss the unopened canonical document; fixture process handling could leave background cargo/node processes.

INDEPENDENT_CHECKS_RUN: direct fixture probe exercised /health and a negative /mt250-fixture/proof?doc_ids=missing-doc lookup outside the visual spec, proving the fixture is reachable and missing canonical documents fail closed with 404.
INDEPENDENT_CHECKS_RUN: process cleanup probe found no leftover mt250_workspace_search_fixture or mt250-workspace-search-real-backend cargo/node processes after the full matrix.

COUNTERFACTUAL_CHECKS: If tests/visual/mt250-workspace-search-real-backend.spec.ts stopped rewriting /workspaces/w1/loom/graph-search to the seeded workspace id, the result assertions would fail because the UI would search the wrong workspace.

COUNTERFACTUAL_CHECKS: If src/backend/handshake_core/src/bin/mt250_workspace_search_fixture.rs fixture_proof stopped reading knowledge_rich_documents and list_kernel_events_for_aggregate, the apply proof would no longer detect missing PostgreSQL writes or missing EventLedger receipts.
BOUNDARY_PROBES: UI-to-API boundary is covered by route-captured source_kinds=document graph-search requests and real api::routes, not mocked search responses.

BOUNDARY_PROBES: writer-to-reader boundary is covered by cancel/apply proof reads from PostgreSQL after UI actions; cancel requires unchanged doc_version/event_count and apply requires doc_version=2 plus KNOWLEDGE_RICH_DOCUMENT_SAVED receipt payloads.
NEGATIVE_PATH_CHECKS: cancel path preserves the exact pre-cancel proof object and writes no EventLedger events.
NEGATIVE_PATH_CHECKS: direct missing proof document lookup returns 404 instead of manufacturing proof for absent canonical state.

INDEPENDENT_FINDINGS: no blocking MT-250 findings remain after the real-backend fixture proof; scoped Cargo.toml diff includes unrelated existing WP-KERNEL-009 additions, so this evidence claims only the mt250_workspace_search_fixture registration and the new MT-250 fixture/spec files.
RESIDUAL_UNCERTAINTY: validator should rerun the same Playwright spec against their chosen reachable PostgreSQL environment before PASS; the current status is READY_FOR_VALIDATION only.
risks: Cargo.toml has unrelated existing uncommitted WP-KERNEL-009 additions in this dirty worktree; MT-250 evidence is scoped to the mt250_workspace_search_fixture bin registration plus src/backend/handshake_core/src/bin/mt250_workspace_search_fixture.rs and tests/visual/mt250-workspace-search-real-backend.spec.ts.
The fixture is validation-only and uses the existing managed/env PostgreSQL path; validator should rerun before marking PASS.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-250 requires a workspace-wide find-in-files surface over the MT-186 search backend with kind/tag/path filters, case/word/regex toggles, click-to-open at match, and safe replace-in-files (per-match preview + confirm, receipts on apply, canonical-set not visible-subset), proven against real PostgreSQL/EventLedger. The implementation (WorkspaceSearchPanel.tsx + real MT-186 backend route + a real PG-backed fixture and end-to-end Playwright spec) delivers all of this with genuine runtime proof, not mocks or scaffolding.
findings: app/src/components/WorkspaceSearchPanel.tsx:531-593 runSearch wires the UI to the real MT-186 backend (searchLoomGraph -> /workspaces/{id}/loom/graph-search) and paginates the canonical result set (SEARCH_PAGE_SIZE=500 loop), passing case_sensitive/whole_word/regex/path/source_kinds/tag_ids server-side. Backend parses all of these in src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/loom.rs:3359-3411 (real route registered at loom.rs:287). Not a mocked search.

Canonical-set rule honored: previewReplace (WorkspaceSearchPanel.tsx:636-666) derives document ids from `results` (the backend hit set via documentIdFromLoomSearchHit), NOT from `visibleResults` (the client-filtered rows at line 426-429). An unopened/hidden doc is still loaded and previewed/replaced. The MT acceptance 'negative test with unloaded doc' is satisfied by the real-backend spec replacing the unopenedDoc.

Replace-in-files is preview+confirm, never blind bulk write: replaceInContent/replaceSegment (lines 249-342) produce per-match before/after context snippets (replacementMatchPreview, MATCH_PREVIEW_CONTEXT_CHARS=24); cancel (lines 828-839) clears preview and writes nothing; apply (679-714) writes only through the real saveRichDocument save path and collects save_receipt_event_id receipts; stale guards on resultSetKey (line 630) and previewPlanKey (line 681).

Click-open lands at the match: openHit (595-620) routes document hits through onOpenDocument carrying EditorFindOptions (query + case/word/regex), enabling the editor FindReplacePanel to select the first match.

Real runtime proof (sub-rule 2): src/backend/handshake_core/src/bin/mt250_workspace_search_fixture.rs boots managed PostgreSQL (ManagedPostgres::ensure_running / POSTGRES_TEST_URL), runs real migrations, seeds via the real KnowledgeStore.create_knowledge_rich_document, and serves the REAL api::routes (loom.rs:242-249), not a mocked search. The proof endpoint reads knowledge_rich_documents and list_kernel_events_for_aggregate (real EventLedger) only to read state back, never to manufacture it.

tests/visual/mt250-workspace-search-real-backend.spec.ts drives the real WorkspaceSearchPanel (built from app via Vite in build_workspace_search_harness.ts -> workspace_search_visual_harness.tsx) through the real backend: asserts both docs returned (source_kinds=document on the wire, line 209-216), unopened doc replaced (lines 230-232), cancel leaves doc_version=1/event_count=0 read from real PG (237-245), apply yields doc_version=2 + real KNOWLEDGE_RICH_DOCUMENT_SAVED receipts (254-264, emitted by the real MT-145 save handler in api/knowledge_documents.rs, only read back by the fixture), and externalRequests===[] (zero external network, line 269).

sub-rule 1: grep over the MT-250 surfaces found no todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'not yet wired'/'deferred to follow-on'/'pending MT-' escapes; the only `placeholder` hits are legitimate HTML input attributes. No SQLite / :memory: authority (DuckDB in-memory at fixture line 222 is the diagnostics flight-recorder, not durable authority).

sub-rule 3: lifecycle.claimed_by=KERNEL_BUILDER, completed_by=null, status=READY_FOR_VALIDATION — no self-certified COMPLETED transition. Aside (non-blocking): packet/MT spec_anchors cite v02.192/v02.193 while SPEC_CURRENT is v02.194 and lifecycle.implementation_commit is null; expected winding-down paperwork drift, not a code defect.
evidence_checked: grep: KNOWLEDGE_RICH_DOCUMENT_SAVED emit sites; placeholder/SQLite grep over MT-250 surfaces; git log for MT-250 commits

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger and/or offline Playwright where the contract names durable state or GUI behavior.
